differential_diagnoses: Bacterial sepsis (e.g., S. pneumoniae, S. aureus)
next_steps: Perform bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) for PJP immunofluorescence, AFB smear/culture, MTB PCR, fungal culture, and CMV PCR
Obtain serum Histoplasma antigen and serum Cryptococcal antigen
Obtain arterial blood gas (ABG) to determine A-a gradient and assess severity of respiratory failure
Order high-resolution computed tomography (HRCT) of the chest
Initiate empiric high-dose trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole and corticosteroids (if PJP suspected)
Obtain blood cultures for bacteria, fungi, and mycobacteria
Monitor respiratory status for potential escalation to non-invasive positive pressure ventilation or intubation
